name: SFF-2026 S-Process Main Round
description: Annual S-Process grant round for AI safety, alignment and governance projects. Evaluation work on frontier model assessment is in-scope as part of the technical AI safety portfolio.
budget_notes: $20M-$40M across all tracks; individual grants vary; Main Round is the largest bucket
eligible_regions: Global (excluding countries considered adversarial to the US)
notes: Applicants must receive a Speculation Grant; >95% of past applicants did. Deadline imminent (~2 weeks).

name: 2026 Science of Trustworthy AI RFP
description: Technical research on foundations of AI safety, interpretability, risk prediction. Explicit pillar on 'advancing the science of evaluations with decision-relevant construct and predictive validity.' Directly funds work on measurement gaps, saturation, and decision-relevance of benchmarks.
budget_notes: Tier 1: up to $1M; Tier 2: $1M-$5M+; 1-3 year projects; indirect costs capped at 10%
notes: Highest-fit opportunity. Directly targets the science-of-evaluations gap the topic asks about.

name: 2026 AI Interpretability RFP (Pilot)
description: Supports interpretability research including a red-team/blue-team competition to detect deceptive behaviors in LLMs. Interpretability-as-evaluation angle: measuring whether models produce deceptive reasoning/communication beyond surface benchmark scores.
budget_notes: Part of Schmidt Sciences' >$1M trustworthy AI grant family; exact cap not published on landing page
notes: Deadline on SmApply portal; confirm before applying.

name: SecureAI: Enhancing the Security, Privacy and Robustness of AI Models and Systems
description: Call focused on resilience of AI systems against adversarial attacks, backdoor injection, data poisoning. Robustness evaluation methodology and red-teaming are in-scope.
notes: Robustness/adversarial evaluation angle. Verify specific topic deadline on EU Funding & Tenders portal.

name: Next-Generation AI Agents for Real-World Applications (Apply AI)
description: Calls for next-gen AI agents with explicit requirement for mechanisms to assess progress, including qualitative/quantitative KPIs, benchmarking and progress monitoring. Evaluation component is mandatory.
budget_notes: Budget per project indicative; part of CL4 2026 Digital calls (total CL4 2026 Digital calls published Jan 2026)
notes: Benchmarking is a required deliverable. Verify deadline and budget envelope on Funding & Tenders portal.

name: DIGITAL-2026-AI-09-VIRTUAL-TESTBEDS-STEP
description: Virtual worlds / XR testbeds under AI Testing & Experimentation Facilities umbrella. Builds large-scale infrastructure for testing AI systems in real-world conditions - evaluation infrastructure for frontier systems in deployment settings.
budget_notes: EUR 8M-9M per project; two testbed networks funded
eligible_regions: EU + associated countries; requires consortium across >=3 Member States
notes: Check EU Funding & Tenders portal for exact deadline.

name: AI Act Technical Assistance for AI Safety (Call for Tenders, incl. Lot 6 Agentic Evaluations)
description: Forthcoming tender supporting monitoring of compliance and assessment of systemic risks from general-purpose AI models. Split into 6 lots including CBRN, cyber, loss of control, manipulation, sociotechnical, and Lot 6 specifically on agentic evaluations.
budget_notes: Budget per lot not yet published on announcement page; tender forthcoming
notes: Procurement tender (not grant). Non-profits can participate via consortium with eligible entities. Very high thematic fit; monitor AI Office page for publication.

name: Foresight Institute AI for Safety & Science Nodes (SF + Berlin)
description: Monthly rolling grants to researchers using AI to advance science and safety. Focus areas include computer security, game theory, automating alignment research, forecasting - evaluation of frontier capabilities and risks in scope. Berlin node opens 1 April 2026, making EU participation straightforward.
budget_notes: $10K-$100K per grant; ~$3M/yr total envelope; includes office/compute
eligible_regions: Global (Berlin node specifically serves EU researchers)

name: Long-Term Future Fund - Speculation Grants & Main Round
description: Rolling Speculation Grants plus annual S-process round. Funds AI existential-risk reduction including technical evaluation work on frontier models and governance-relevant evaluations.
budget_notes: Historically >$20M distributed to AI risk over 5 years; grants vary widely
notes: Rolling speculation grants mean applications accepted year-round.

name: AI Risk Mitigation Fund (ARM Fund)
organization: Effective Ventures Foundation USA / ARM Fund
description: Grants in technical research, policy and talent programmes to reduce catastrophic risks from advanced AI. Technical alignment and evaluation research explicitly in scope.
budget_notes: Individual grant sizes not published on landing page; fiscally sponsored
notes: Confirm specific budget caps before applying.
